title: pittosporum senacia subsp. wrightii
intro: Pittosporum senacia subsp. wrightii is a plant species belonging to the Pittosporaceae family, commonly known as the forget-me-not tree or Kohuhu in Maori language. It is native to New Zealand and has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine to treat a variety of ailments. The plant has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties and health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Pittosporum senacia subsp. wrightii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
